title: 10 best herbal essential oils for upset stomach
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for upset stomach are natural remedies extracted from plants that help alleviate digestive discomfort and ease abdominal pain. These oils, including Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Angelica archangelica, Peumus boldus, and Matricaria chamomilla, offer relief from digestive issues. If left untreated, upset stomach can lead to missed work or school, social isolation, and a decline in overall well-being, negatively impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural digestive properties. The oil's active compound, menthol, stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. It also helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms that can contribute to discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil's calming effects on the gut may help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive stress, providing quick relief from an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil's primary constituent, menthol, stimulates digestive enzymes and increases gastric motility, helping to relieve symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and indigestion.

Antispasmodic: The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the smooth muscle lining of the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders.

Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint oil's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help alleviate digestive issues and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with upset stomach.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach lining and ease symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves from your garden or obtain them from a trusted supplier.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and mild soap to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill the washed leaves in a copper alembic still at 100°c for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue using a separating funnel and collect it in a dark glass bottle.
Store the extracted essential oil in a cool place away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with upset stomach because it contains glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When ingested, glycyrrhizin soothes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach. Additionally, licorice oil's warm, gentle nature can calm cramps and spasms in the abdominal area, providing quick relief from indigestion, nausea, and bloating. Its ability to ease digestive discomfort makes it a popular natural remedy for calming the stomach and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice essential oil contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of IBS and other stomach issues.

Soothers and calms stomach acid: The essential oil's flavonoids and saponins have a calming effect on stomach acidity, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn, acid reflux, and indigestion.

Antispasmodic properties: Licorice essential oil has been shown to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, relieving cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with IBS, constipation, and other stomach issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to create medicinal essential oils that soothe digestive issues and alleviate nausea due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the licorice plant are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to calm digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days.
Crush 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the crushed root pieces with 500ml of water at 60-70°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through coffee filters or cotton cloth into dark glass bottles.
Store the finished herbal glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with upset stomach because it contains natural compounds that stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea. The oil's warming properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as heartburn and diarrhea. This natural remedy can be used topically or inhaled to provide quick and effective relief from upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Digestive stimulant property: Ginger has natural digestive stimulant properties that can help increase gastric motility, reduce nausea and vomiting, and improve digestion by stimulating the release of stomach acid and enzymes.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in herbal ginger essential oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion, bloating, and gas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the ginger plant, and they are rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to create essential oils that can help soothe nausea, reduce inflammation, and ease symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root and rinse it thoroughly under cold running water.
Cut the ginger into small pieces and steam distill using 20g of material per liter of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.
Use the essential oil as needed by diluting it with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1% essential oil per 99% carrier oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining helps alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon essential oils contains cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to slow down digestion and help regulate bowel movements, providing relief from upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to upset stomach symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation in the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other conditions characterized by chronic inflammation, leading to improved digestion and reduced discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: Helps to combat harmful bacteria and fungi that can contribute to digestive issues such as diarrhea, stomach ulcers, and food poisoning, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing the risk of infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good sources of bioactive compounds that help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Roots: The roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal disorders, including upset stomach and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves in the early morning ensuring optimal oil content and minimal contamination.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 3 hours at 100°c using 10% water to obtain a high-quality essential oil.
Filter the distilled oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and sediment within 30 minutes of distillation.
Store the filtered oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 6 months.
Use the final product as is or blend with other oils at a ratio of 1:2 cinnamomum verum to carrier oil for therapeutic applications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and relieve cramps. Its anise-like scent also stimulates the release of digestive enzymes, promoting a healthy gut environment and alleviating symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel trapped air and reduce discomfort, providing quick relief from stomach upset and allowing for a smoother digestive process.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil helps to calm digestive discomforts by releasing gas from the stomach, alleviating cramps, bloating, and flatulence.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel essential oil reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain associated with upset stomach.
Antispasmodic property: Fennel essential oil relaxes the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, calming spasms and cramps that can contribute to stomach discomfort, diarrhea, or constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to soothe digestive issues, including upset stomachs, due to their gentle anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.

Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used as a natural remedy for indigestion, nausea, and other digestive disorders, as they contain essential oils that can help relax the muscles of the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in small batches using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a copper alembic still using pure water at atmospheric pressure for 4 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillation process by decanting it into a clean glass vial within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities before storing it in an amber glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with upset stomach because it contains a compound called carvone, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When inhaled or digested, carvone helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to upset stomach symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain. Additionally, caraway essential oils have been shown to stimulate digestion and improve bowel function, promoting a healthy gut environment and relieving symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Caraway essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to relieve bloating, gas, and indigestion by reducing the formation of gas in the intestines.
Antispasmodic property: The oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestinal tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to stomach upset and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory property: Caraway essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile compounds, caraway roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them effective in soothing upset stomachs.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a high concentration of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Dry the carum carvi seeds in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Crush the dried seeds into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part crushed carum carvi with 4 parts carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, and stir well.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily, before straining and discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural soothing properties, which help to calm digestive issues such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea. The oil's gentle warming action can ease discomfort and reduce inflammation in the abdominal area, providing relief from nausea and indigestion. Additionally, sweet marjoram has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive discomfort caused by an upset stomach, such as cramps, bloating, and gas.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of marjoram oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders.

Digestive stimulant: Marjoram oil's digestive stimulant properties can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and vomiting by stimulating the production of bile and gastric juices.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sweet marjoram plant are used to create essential oils that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of upset stomach.
Leaves: Sweet marjoram leaves are a popular choice for making essential oils, as they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stomach discomfort and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marjoram leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested leaves in small batches using a food dehydrator for 6 hours.
Combine one cup of dried marjoram leaves with 400 milliliters of carrier oil like sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steam distill the mixture for 30 minutes to extract essential oils and isolate compounds.
Filter and decant the resulting blend, storing it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural calming and soothing properties. The herb has been used for centuries to ease digestive issues, and its oil form allows for easy absorption into the body. When inhaled or applied topically, lemon balm essential oil can help relax the stomach muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate cramps, making it a popular remedy for indigestion, bloating, and other stomach discomforts.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm essential oil helps to relax and calm spasming muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain high levels of citral, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing upset stomachs.
Roots: The roots of lemon balm are known to have antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves in early morning or late afternoon to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours and then store them in airtight containers.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours and 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle to remove impurities and preserve quality.
Store the final melissa officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 12 months with a 5% dilution of jojoba oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When ingested or inhaled, chamomile oil calms the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause discomfort, bloating, and cramps. It also helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, allowing for a smoother digestion process and alleviating symptoms such as nausea and indigestion. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system may help reduce anxiety and stress-related digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil helps to relax and calm digestive spasms, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms like bloating, gas, and abdominal pain.
Sedative property: Chamomile essential oil has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which helps to calm stress and anxiety-related stomach issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and soothing essential oil that helps to ease digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Flowers: The flowers contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce spasms and cramps in the stomach, providing relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or early morning when they are still dewy.
Wash the harvested flowers gently with cold running water and pat dry with a soft cloth.
Combine 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily for eight minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice essential oils helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eugenol and caryophyllene, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation and spasms, and ease cramps. Additionally, allspice oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach-related issues. By promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort, allspice essential oils provide natural relief from upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Allspice essential oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating by promoting proper digestion.
Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of allspice essential oil can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to conditions like gastritis and ulcers.

Antispasmodic: Allspice essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds of the allspice plant are used to make medicinal essential oils because they contain a high concentration of aromatic compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of allspice have been traditionally used to make medicinal essential oils for upset stomach due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the abdominal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pimenta dioica leaves by cutting fresh stems in the morning for maximum potency and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or until they crumble easily.
Combine 30g of dried pimenta dioica leaves with 300ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar and seal tightly for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids, and let it steep for an additional 4 days.
Distill the liquid using a still or a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil, collecting it in a separate container within 30 minutes of distillation completion.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upset stomach with herbal essential oils is by blending Peppermint, Ginger, and Fennel oils. Add a few drops of each oil to a carrier oil like Coconut or Olive oil and massage onto the abdomen. This combination helps ease digestive discomfort, reduces nausea, and alleviates bloating. Regular use can promote healthy digestion and alleviate stomach upset symptoms.
